description_long: This is a single-story residential house with a moderately sloped, shingled gable roof and a rectangular footprint. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted concrete, and the roof extends to form a covered porch supported by square columns at the front. The house features a simple window arrangement, with small rectangular windows on the sides and a larger window or glass door at the rear. The main entryway is recessed beneath the porch, providing shelter and a welcoming approach. The surrounding area is paved, and the house is flanked by display boards, suggesting it may be part of a model home or exhibition setting.
description: A single-story house with a gable shingled roof, light-colored walls, and a covered front porch supported by columns. Windows are modestly sized and symmetrically placed, with a simple, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entryway, and two supporting columns beneath the gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, uninterrupted wall with a single small window and the full expanse of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large window or glass door and the back elevation of the house, with display boards visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another small window and the roof overhang, with the porch partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry beneath a covered porch with square columns and a simple door.
